title: Kenyan Chakula ya Kukunja
description: A Kenyan-inspired dish with a floral and flaky pastry crust filled with spicy braised meat, served as a dinner option.

ingredients.name: Flour (All-purpose, high-protein)
ingredients.notes: Use a high-protein flour for better texture

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Use 2-3 cloves if you prefer a stronger garlic flavor

ingredients.name: Tomato, diced
ingredients.notes: Use any variety of tomato you like

ingredients.name: Chilli powder
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, mild to medium-hot chilli powder

ingredients.name: Curry powder
ingredients.notes: Use a mild to medium-hot curry powder for added depth

ingredients.name: Black pepper, freshly ground
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground black pepper for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Braising liquid (beef broth or water)
ingredients.notes: Use beef broth for added richness

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and black pepper. Gradually add ghee or oil and mix until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat. Add sliced onion and cook until it's lightly browned.
Add minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add diced tomato and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to break down.
Add chilli powder, curry powder, and braising liquid. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, until the sauce has thickened.
Roll out the rested dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Place the dough in a 9x13 inch baking dish and brush the edges with ghee or oil.
Spoon the braised meat mixture into the pastry-lined dish and cover with another sheet of pastry.
Brush the top with ghee or oil and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
